title: Lomaria scandens
intro: Lomaria scandens, also known as the climbing fern or climbing lomaria, is a species of fern native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and Australia, characterized by its delicate, lacy fronds and climbing habit. The plant is valued for its traditional medicinal uses, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help to relieve congestion and soothe the airways. The bioactive constituents of Lomaria scandens include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Lomaria scandens is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, which can be used to treat respiratory problems, as well as being used in combination with other herbs to support overall health and well-being.
